page: Microsoft Azure D8_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dv3 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor operating at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance provides 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core, utilizing Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization. It features 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 205 GB of local storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the D8_v3 exhibits performance characteristics that lean toward the lower tiers of current cloud offerings. Its single-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the bottom 10%, while multi-core performance sits in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only speeds of 300.18 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 36.83 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, both ranking in the bottom 25%. Similarly, database operations and Redis SET performance are positioned in the bottom 25%. However, the server achieves average performance in LLM text generation for small 135M models, reaching 85.57 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D8_v3 represents an older hardware generation that offers a balanced resource density of compute and memory at a lower entry point compared to newer generations. The inclusion of 205 GB of local storage provides value for workloads requiring temporary disk space. Given its modest benchmark results, this instance is best suited for general-purpose workloads with low-to-moderate resource demands, such as basic web serving, development and testing environments, lightweight database operations, and small-scale text processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D8_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dv3 family, configured with 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz, this x86_64 instance features 4 physical cores with hyper-threading and includes 205 GB of local storage. Benchmark data indicates that the D8_v3 generally delivers entry-level performance, with single-core CPU and multi-core CPU metrics placing in the bottom 10% and 25% respectively. Memory bandwidth and database operations also rank in the lower quartiles, though small-model LLM text generation achieves average speeds of 85.57 tokens per second. Given these performance trade-offs, the D8_v3 is best utilized for cost-conscious, moderate-demand workloads such as development environments, basic web serving, and lightweight database tasks where high-performance compute is not a primary requirement.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and 205 GB of local storage, suitable for moderate development and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8_v3 is a general-purpose VM with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 205 GB local storage for moderate workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine with dedicated Intel Xeon compute and local storage for moderate, cost-conscious workloads.
bullet_points: Features 8 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor at 2.3 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM and 205 GB of local storage
Delivers entry-level single-core and multi-core CPU performance
Achieves average text generation speeds of 85.57 tokens/sec on small LLM models
Best suited for development environments, basic web serving, and light database tasks
